questions.q: The Governor is the constitutional head of which level of government?
questions.explanation: The Governor is the constitutional head of a state.

questions.q: Who appoints the Governor of a state?
questions.explanation: The Governor of a state is appointed by the President of India.

questions.q: On whose advice does the Governor normally act in most matters?
questions.explanation: The Governor normally acts on the advice of the state Council of Ministers.

questions.q: At the state level, the Governor performs a role analogous to which office at the Union level?
questions.explanation: The Governor at the state level performs a role analogous to that of the President at the Union level.

questions.q: Who appoints the Chief Minister of a state?
questions.explanation: The Governor appoints the Chief Minister of the state.

questions.q: The Governor holds office during the pleasure of whom?
questions.explanation: The Governor holds office during the pleasure of the President.

questions.q: A bill passed by a state legislature generally becomes law after assent is given by whom?
questions.explanation: A bill passed by the state legislature requires the Governor's assent to become law.

questions.q: The Governor is the head of which arm of a state's government?
questions.explanation: The Governor is the constitutional head of the state's executive.

questions.q: What is the normal term of office of a Governor?
questions.explanation: A Governor normally holds office for a term of five years, subject to the pleasure of the President.

questions.q: The Governor addresses the state legislature and gives assent to bills as part of which kind of functions?
questions.explanation: The Governor performs executive and legislative functions, including addressing the legislature and assenting to bills.

questions.q: Which Article of the Constitution provides that there shall be a Governor for each state?
questions.explanation: Article 153 provides that there shall be a Governor for each state.

questions.q: What is the minimum age requirement to be appointed Governor of a state?
questions.explanation: A person must have completed the age of 35 years to be eligible for appointment as Governor.

questions.q: Under the Constitution, the executive power of a state is vested in whom?
questions.explanation: The executive power of a state is vested in the Governor and exercised by the Governor directly or through subordinate officers.

questions.q: To whom does the Governor address a letter of resignation?
questions.explanation: The Governor may resign by writing addressed to the President of India.

questions.q: When a Governor reserves a bill for the consideration of the President, that action falls under which kind of power?
questions.explanation: The Governor may reserve certain bills for the President's consideration, a power exercised in the legislative process at times in discretion.

questions.q: Can the same person be appointed Governor of more than one state at the same time?
questions.explanation: The Constitution permits the same person to be appointed Governor of two or more states.

questions.q: Which Article empowers the Governor to grant pardons, reprieves and remissions for offences against state laws?
questions.explanation: Article 161 gives the Governor the power to grant pardons, reprieves and remissions for offences against laws relating to state matters.

questions.q: Who administers the oath of office to the Governor of a state?
options: The Chief Justice of the High Court having jurisdiction over that state
questions.explanation: The Governor's oath of office is administered by the Chief Justice of the High Court having jurisdiction over the state.

questions.q: The salary and allowances of the Governor are charged on which fund?
options: The Consolidated Fund of the State
questions.explanation: The salary and allowances of the Governor are charged on the Consolidated Fund of the State.

questions.q: Must a person be a resident of the state to be appointed its Governor?
options: Yes, residence in the state is mandatory
No, residence in the state is not a requirement
questions.explanation: There is no constitutional requirement that the Governor be a resident of the state to which appointed.

questions.q: Provisions relating to the Governor of a state are found mainly in which Part of the Constitution?
questions.explanation: Part VI of the Constitution deals with the states, including the office of Governor.

questions.q: When the office of Governor falls vacant, who customarily discharges its functions until a new Governor is appointed?
options: The Chief Justice of the relevant High Court, as appointed by the President
questions.explanation: The President may appoint the Chief Justice of the relevant High Court to discharge the Governor's functions during a vacancy.

questions.q: A bill assented to by the Governor may, in certain cases, instead be reserved for the assent of whom?
options: The Speaker of the Lok Sabha
questions.explanation: Under Article 200, the Governor may reserve a bill for the assent of the President in certain situations.

questions.q: The qualifications, oath and conditions of the Governor's office are largely modelled on which other constitutional office?
options: The Speaker of the Lok Sabha
questions.explanation: Many provisions on the Governor's qualifications and office parallel those of the President of India.

questions.q: A Governor whose term has expired continues to hold office until when?
options: Until a successor enters upon the office
questions.explanation: A Governor continues to hold office, notwithstanding the expiry of the term, until a successor enters upon the office.

questions.q: Under Article 213, the Governor has the power to promulgate which kind of instrument when the state legislature is not in session?
questions.explanation: Article 213 empowers the Governor to promulgate ordinances when the state legislature is not in session.

questions.q: The Governor appoints which state law officer who advises the state government?
questions.explanation: The Governor appoints the Advocate General of the state, the chief law officer of the state government.

questions.q: Is a Governor entitled to the immunity from court proceedings for acts done in the exercise of official powers, similar to the President?
options: Yes, similar immunity is provided under Article 361
questions.explanation: Article 361 extends to the Governor an immunity similar to that of the President for official acts and personal proceedings during the term.

questions.q: In a state that has a Legislative Council, members of the Council partly include those nominated by whom?
questions.explanation: In states with a Legislative Council, the Governor nominates a portion of the members, for example those with special knowledge or experience.

questions.q: The Lieutenant Governor heads which kind of territory, as distinct from a state's Governor?
questions.explanation: Certain union territories are headed by a Lieutenant Governor or Administrator rather than by a state Governor.
